    • 防饮与从容赋诗 - Means 'to drink and compose poetry leisurely.' '防饮' (Fangyin) means to drink, and '从容赋诗' (Congrong fushi) means to compose poetry leisurely.
    • 适闻判官与诸贤作诗何得见某而罢 - Means 'just now I heard that the judge and the gentlemen were composing poetry, why did they stop when they saw me?' '判官' (Panguan) refers to a judge or official, and '诸贤' (Zhuxian) means the gentlemen.
    • 遽令却取笔砚复以防牋数十幅散于座 - Means 'he immediately ordered them to take back the writing tools and scatter dozens of sheets of paper on the seat.' '遽令' (Juling) means immediately ordered, '却取' (Quequ) means to take back, '笔砚' (Biyi) means writing tools, '防牋' (Fangjian) means sheets of paper, and '散于座' (San yu zuo) means to scatter on the seat.
    • 从事礼为揖 - Means 'the official performed a courtesy bow.' '礼为揖' (Li wei yi) means to perform a courtesy bow.
    • 前某以韬略发迹未尝留心章句 - Means 'formerly, I rose to prominence through strategy and never paid attention to literary phrases.' '韬略' (Taolue) means strategy, '发迹' (Faji) means to rise to prominence, '未尝' (Weicang) means never, and '留心章句' (Liu xin zhangju) means to pay attention to literary phrases.
    • 今日陪奉英髦不免亦陈愚恳 - Means 'today, accompanying the talented and wise, I cannot help but express my humble opinion.' '英髦' (Yingmao) means talented and wise, '不免' (Bumian) means cannot help but, '陈' (Chen) means to express, '愚恳' (Yukeng) means humble opinion.
    • 遂乃引援毫顷刻而就 - Means 'he then took up the brush and wrote it in a moment.' '引' (Yin shi) means to take up the brush, '援毫' (Yuan hao) means to take up the brush, '顷刻' (Qingke) means in a moment, and '而就' (Er jiu) means to complete.
    • 平生弓劒自相随刚郎官遣作诗 - Means 'throughout life, the bow and sword have been with me, and as a young official, I was ordered to write poetry.' '弓劒' (Gongjian) means bow and sword, '自相随' (Zixiang sui) means to be with me, '刚' (Gang zhu) means young, '郎官' (Langguan) means a young official, and '遣作诗' (Qian zuoshi) means to be ordered to write poetry.
    • 江南花柳从君咏塞北烟尘我自知 - Means 'the flowers and willows of the south can be sung by you, but the dust of the north I know well.' '江南' (Jiangnan) means the south, '花柳' (Hualiu) means flowers and willows, '从君咏' (Cong jun yong) means can be sung by you, '塞北' (Saibei) means the north, '烟尘' (Yanchen) means dust, and '我自知' (Wo zizhi) means I know well.
    • 四座览之惊叹无已咸云忠烈词彩虽曹景宗贺 - Means 'all present were amazed and exclaimed without end, all saying that the loyal and valiant words and colors are even more splendid than those of Cao Jingzong and He.' '四座' (Sizuo) means all present, '览之' (Lan zhi) means to look at it, '惊叹无已' (Jian tan wu yi) means amazed and exclaimed without end, '咸云' (Xian yun) means all say, '忠烈' (Zhonglie) means loyal and valiant, '词彩' (Cicai) means words and colors, '虽' (Sui) means even, '曹景宗' (Cao Jingzong) and '贺' (He) are historical figures known for their literary talents.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 狄惟谦 - A historical figure, likely a noble and loyal official.
    • 晋祠 - A famous temple in Shanxi Province dedicated to the memory of Duke Wen of Jin.
    • 邺县 - An ancient county in Henan Province, known for its historical significance.
    • 曹景宗 - A historical figure known for his military and literary talents.
    • 贺 - Another historical figure known for his literary talents.
    • 朱绂 - A red sash worn by officials, symbolizing high rank.
    • 铜章 - A copper seal, often given as a symbol of authority or recognition.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • The long sentence '勑书云狄惟谦剧邑良才忠臣华胄覩此天厉将瘅下民当请祷于晋祠投巫于邺县曝山椒之畏景事等焚躯起天际之油云法同翦爪遂使旱风潜息甘泽旋流天心犹鉴于克诚余志岂忘于褒善特颁朱绂俾耀铜章勿替令名更昭殊绩' must be read as a continuous narrative, describing the actions and rewards of Didi Weiqian.

    Imperial Decree: Didi Weiqian, a man of noble lineage, talent, and loyalty, has witnessed this divine scourge that will afflict the common people. He should pray at the Jinci Temple, cast the witch into Yexian, expose himself to the terrifying sun, and perform acts equivalent to burning his body to summon dark, heavy clouds from the horizon. The method is the same as cutting one's nails, thereby causing the drought winds to cease and the sweet rain to flow again. The heart of heaven still recognizes his sincerity. My intention is not to forget his good deeds, but to specially grant him a red sash and a copper seal to shine, so that his good name is not forgotten and his extraordinary achievements are further highlighted.

    Wang Shizhong's Poem

    Wang Shizhong, known as Zhixing, is a man of extraordinary military strategy and talent. He was initially appointed as the governor of Xu Prefecture, where his great talent and noble character earned him a reputation in the world. Once the military office was established, all those appointed were scholars. He served in the mission office, where he drank and composed poetry leisurely. Soon, his poetry reached the ears of the Grand Master, who then summoned the guards and officials to gather. Wang Shizhong, however, dismissed the writing tools and simply welcomed them with cups and plates. After a long while, the Grand Master asked, "Just now I heard that the judge and the gentlemen were composing poetry, why did they stop when they saw me?" He immediately ordered them to take back the writing tools and scatter dozens of sheets of paper on the seat. The officials looked at each other hesitantly, waiting for the wine to be served and the music to begin. The Grand Master said again, "I wanted to see the creation, not to drink." At that time, a minor official also placed sheets of paper before the Grand Master. Wang Shizhong performed a courtesy bow. The Grand Master said, "Formerly, I rose to prominence through strategy and never paid attention to literary phrases. Today, accompanying the talented and wise, I cannot help but express my humble opinion." He then took up the brush and wrote it in a moment:

    "Throughout life, the bow and sword have been with me, and as a young official, I was ordered to write poetry. The flowers and willows of the south can be sung by you, but the dust of the north I know well."

    All present were amazed and exclaimed without end, all saying that the loyal and valiant words and colors are even more splendid than those of Cao Jingzong and He. [30]

    弼无以加也【曹景宗于御座探韵赋诗云去时儿女悲归来笳鼓竞借问路傍人何如霍去病宋帝览之称赏无已又隋将贺弼赠源雄诗云交河骠骑幕合浦伏波营莫使骐驎上无我二人名】时文人张祜亦预此筵监军谓之曰覩兹盛事岂得无言祜即席为诗以献云古来英杰动寰区武徳文经未有余王氏柱天勲业外李陵章句右军书王公览之笑曰襃餙之词可谓无所爱惜左右或言曰书生之徒务为谄佞王公叱之曰有人道我恶汝軰又肯否张秀才海内知名篇什岂易得天下人闻且以为王智兴乐善矣留驻数月赠行以绢千匹其后移镇蒲津子晏平仗节灵武四逺多士翕然归向风烈遗芳迄于今日 [31]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [31]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 弼无以加也 - A phrase meaning 'there is no way to surpass him,' indicating that someone's achievements are unmatched.
    • 去时儿女悲，归来笳鼓竞 - A couplet from a poem by Cao Jingzong, expressing the emotional contrast between departure and return.
    • 借问路傍人，何如霍去病 - A line from the poem, asking passersby how they compare to Huo Qubing, a famous Han dynasty general.
    • 交河骠骑幕，合浦伏波营 - Refers to military camps, with '骠骑' and '伏波' being titles of famous generals.
    • 莫使骐驎上，无我二人名 - A line from a poem by He Xianbi, expressing the desire for fame and recognition.
    • 覩兹盛事，岂得无言 - A phrase meaning 'seeing such a grand event, how could one remain silent?'
    • 古来英杰动寰区 - A line from a poem by Zhang Hu, praising the impact of historical figures.
    • 武德文经未有余 - A line from the same poem, suggesting that the achievements in both military and literary fields are unmatched.
    • 王氏柱天勲业外 - Refers to the Wáng family's significant contributions and achievements.
    • 李陵章句右军书 - Refers to Li Ling's literary works and the calligraphy of Wang Xizhi.
    • 襃餙之词 - Means 'praising and embellishing words,' indicating flattery.
    • 无所爱惜 - Means 'without holding back,' suggesting full praise.
    • 务为谄佞 - Means 'to engage in flattery,' indicating insincere praise.
    • 有人道我恶汝軰 - A phrase meaning 'if someone says I dislike you,' showing a threat.
    • 风烈遗芳 - Means 'the lasting influence and fragrance,' indicating the enduring legacy.
    • 迄于今日 - Means 'until today,' indicating the continuity of the legacy.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 曹景宗 - A famous general of the Southern Qi and Liang dynasties.
    • 宋帝 - Refers to Emperor Wu of Liang, who was known for his appreciation of poetry.
    • 贺若弼 - A famous general of the Sui dynasty.
    • 源雄 - A military officer and poet of the Sui dynasty.
    • 张祜 - A renowned poet of the Tang dynasty.
    • 王智兴 - A military general of the Tang dynasty.
    • 灵武 - A place name, referring to a region in present-day Ningxia.
    • 蒲津 - A place name, referring to a region in present-day Shanxi.
    • 霍去病 - A famous Han dynasty general known for his military exploits.
    • 李陵 - A Han dynasty general and poet.
    • 王羲之 - A famous calligrapher of the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 王晏平 - A military general of the Tang dynasty.
    • 王智兴 - A military general of the Tang dynasty.

    Proper Nouns

    • 曹景宗 - A famous general of the Southern Qi and Liang dynasties.
    • 宋帝 - Refers to Emperor Wu of Liang.
    • 贺若弼 - A famous general of the Sui dynasty.
    • 源雄 - A military officer and poet of the Sui dynasty.
    • 张祜 - A renowned poet of the Tang dynasty.
    • 王智兴 - A military general of the Tang dynasty.
    • 灵武 - A place name, referring to a region in present-day Ningxia.
    • 蒲津 - A place name, referring to a region in present-day Shanxi.
    • 霍去病 - A famous Han dynasty general.
    • 李陵 - A Han dynasty general and poet.
    • 王羲之 - A famous calligrapher of the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 王晏平 - A military general of the Tang dynasty.
    • 王智兴 - A military general of the Tang dynasty.

    There is no way to surpass him. [Cao Jingzong composed a poem at the imperial court, which read: "When departing, the children are sad; when returning, the drums and horns are loud. Asking the passerby, how does it compare to Huo Qubing?" Emperor Wu of Liang was so pleased that he could not stop praising it. Another poem by the Sui general He Xianbi, given to Yuan Xiong, read: "The military camps of the River Jiao and the camp of the Pacifier of the South Sea. Do not let the Qilin ride without the names of the two of us." At the same time, the poet Zhang Hu also attended this banquet. The military governor said to him, "Seeing such a grand event, how could one remain silent?" Zhang Hu immediately composed a poem to present to him, which read: "From ancient times, the heroes have moved the world. In martial virtue and literary classics, there is none left. Outside the achievements of the Wang family, the writings of Li Ling and the calligraphy of Wang Xizhi." The general smiled and said, "Words of praise and embellishment can be said without holding back." Someone on the side said, "The students of the scholars are only engaged in flattery." The general scolded them, saying, "If someone says I dislike you, will you still do so?" Zhang Hu, a famous scholar of the Tang dynasty, was known for his poems. It was not easy to get such poems. When the people heard of it, they thought that General Wang Zhixing was a lover of good deeds. He stayed for several months, and presented him with a thousand bolts of silk as a gift. Later, he moved to Puzhen, and his son Yanping held a ceremonial staff in Lingwu. Many scholars from afar flocked to him, and his influence and legacy continued until today.] [31]

    道流相夏侯谯公
    　　张侍郎为河阳乌司徒从事同幕皆至有道士殷九霞来自青城山有知人之鉴乌公问以年寿官禄九霞曰司徒贵极藩服所望者秉持钧轴建茅土惟在保守勲庸苞贮仁义享福隆厚殊不可涯既而遍问賔僚九霞曰其间必有台辅时乌公器重裴副使应声曰裴中丞是宰相否九霞曰以目前人事言之当如尊防以某所观即不在此时夏侯相国为馆驿廵官形质低悴乌因戏曰裴副使不作宰相莫是夏侯廵官否对曰司徒所言是矣乌公抚掌而笑曰尊师莫错否九霞曰某山野之人早修直道无意于名宦金玉葢以所见任直而道耳乌公曰如此则非某所知也然其次贵达者为谁曰张支使虽不居廊庙履清途亦至 [32]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [32]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 道流相 - A title or role that combines religious and political authority, possibly referring to a Taoist or Buddhist monk in a high-ranking position.
    • 青城山 - A famous Taoist mountain in Sichuan, known for its spiritual significance.
    • 知人之鉴 - The ability to discern or predict a person's future based on their character or appearance.
    • 贵极藩服 - To reach the highest rank within a feudal system.
    • 秉持钧轴 - To hold a position of great power or authority, often in governance.
    • 建茅土 - To be granted a fiefdom or territory, symbolizing high status.
    • 勋庸苞贮仁义 - To accumulate great achievements while embodying virtue and righteousness.
    • 享福隆厚 - To enjoy a life of great prosperity and happiness.
    • 殊不可涯 - To be without limit or boundary, often used to describe something extraordinary.
    • 台辅 - A high-ranking official, often referring to a prime minister or chancellor.
    • 宰相 - The highest-ranking official in the government, equivalent to a prime minister.
    • 山野之人 - A person from the countryside or a hermit, often used to describe someone who is simple or unworldly.
    • 直道 - To follow a straightforward or righteous path.
    • 无意于名宦 - To have no desire for fame or official positions.
    • 金玉 - Precious metals and jewels, often used metaphorically to describe valuable or important things.
    • 形质低悴 - To appear weak or unimpressive in physical appearance.
    • 抚掌而笑 - To clap one's hands in laughter, indicating amusement or approval.
    • 莫错否 - A rhetorical question meaning 'could you be mistaken?'
    • 山野之人早修直道 - A hermit who has long followed a righteous path.
    • 无意于名宦金玉 - Having no desire for fame, official positions, or material wealth.
    • 以所见任直而道耳 - To speak based on what is seen and heard, following a straightforward path.
    • 次贵达者 - The next most important or successful person.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 道流相 - A title or role that combines religious and political authority, possibly referring to a Taoist or Buddhist monk in a high-ranking position.
    • 夏侯谯公 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official or dignitary, possibly a Taoist or Buddhist monk.
    • 张侍郎 - A high-ranking official, likely a member of the imperial bureaucracy.
    • 河阳乌司徒 - A high-ranking official, possibly a governor or military leader.
    • 殷九霞 - A Taoist or Buddhist monk known for his ability to predict the future.
    • 青城山 - A famous Taoist mountain in Sichuan, known for its spiritual significance.
    • 宰相 - The highest-ranking official in the government, equivalent to a prime minister.
    • 山野之人 - A person from the countryside or a hermit, often used to describe someone who is simple or unworldly.
    • 直道 - To follow a straightforward or righteous path.
    • 无意于名宦金玉 - Having no desire for fame, official positions, or material wealth.
    • 以所见任直而道耳 - To speak based on what is seen and heard, following a straightforward path.

    Proper Nouns

    • 道流相 - A title or role that combines religious and political authority.
    • 夏侯谯公 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official or dignitary.
    • 张侍郎 - A high-ranking official, likely a member of the imperial bureaucracy.
    • 河阳乌司徒 - A high-ranking official, possibly a governor or military leader.
    • 殷九霞 - A Taoist or Buddhist monk known for his ability to predict the future.
    • 青城山 - A famous Taoist mountain in Sichuan, known for its spiritual significance.
    • 宰相 - The highest-ranking official in the government, equivalent to a prime minister.
    • 山野之人 - A person from the countryside or a hermit, often used to describe someone who is simple or unworldly.
    • 直道 - To follow a straightforward or righteous path.
    • 无意于名宦金玉 - Having no desire for fame, official positions, or material wealth.
    • 以所见任直而道耳 - To speak based on what is seen and heard, following a straightforward path.
    • 次贵达者 - The next most important or successful person.

    The High Official and Taoist Monk Master Xiahou of Qiao

    Zhang the Attendant, serving as an assistant to the Governor of Heyang, Wu Sitan, gathered together with other officials. Among them was a Taoist monk named Yin Jiuxia, who had come from Mount Qingcheng and was known for his ability to discern the future. Wu Sitan asked him about the longevity and official rank of those present. Yin Jiuxia replied, "The Governor has reached the highest rank within the feudal system. What he aspires to is to hold a position of great power and to be granted a fiefdom, but this can only be achieved by conserving his achievements, embodying virtue and righteousness, and enjoying a life of great prosperity and happiness, which is without limit." Afterward, Wu Sitan asked Yin Jiuxia about the others. Yin Jiuxia said, "Among them, there must be someone who will become a high-ranking official." At that time, Wu Sitan held in high regard Pei the Deputy Commander. He asked, "Is Pei the Governor going to become a prime minister?" Yin Jiuxia replied, "Based on current events and human affairs, it should be as you say. But according to my observation, it is not so." At that moment, Xiahou the Prime Minister was serving as an inspector at the guesthouse. He appeared weak and unimpressive in physical appearance. Wu Sitan then joked, "Is it not Pei the Deputy Commander who will not become a prime minister, but rather Xiahou the Inspector?" The Deputy Commander replied, "What the Governor says is correct." Wu Sitan clapped his hands and laughed, "Could you be mistaken, Master?" Yin Jiuxia said, "I am a simple hermit who has long followed a righteous path and has no desire for fame, official positions, or material wealth. I speak based on what I see and hear, following a straightforward path." Wu Sitan said, "In that case, it is beyond my knowledge. But who is the next most important person?" Yin Jiuxia replied, "Zhang the Attendant, though he will not hold high office, will still achieve something significant." [32]

    荣显既出遂造张侍郎所居従容谓曰支使神骨清爽气韵髙邈以绂冕累身至于三二十年居于世俗傥能摆脱嚣俗相逐学道即三十年内白日升天某之此行非有尘虑实亦访寻修眞之士耳然阅人甚多无如支使者张以其言意浮阔但唯之而已将去复来情甚恳至审知张意不颇甚嗟惜因留药数粒并黄书一缄而别去云药数粒服之可以无疾书纪宦途所得每一迁转密自啓之书穷之辰当复相忆其后谯公显赫令名再居鼎张果践朝列出入台省佩服朱紫廉察数州书载之言靡不详悉年及三纪时为户部侍郎书之所存葢亦无几虽名位通显而齿发衰退每言道流之事话于亲友追想其风莫能及矣 [33]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [33]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 白日升天 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'ascending to heaven during the day,' often used to describe achieving immortality.
    • 修眞 - Refers to cultivating the Dao or practicing immortality.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 張侍郎 - Title for a high-ranking official; '張' is likely a person's name, and '侍郎' refers to a vice minister.
    • 户部侍郎 - High-ranking official in charge of the Ministry of Revenue.
    • 道流 - Refers to those who follow the Dao or practice immortality.

    Proper Nouns

    • 榮顯 - Likely refers to a person of high status or reputation.
    • 支使 - Likely a title or name.
    • 黄书 - Likely a type of book or document.
    • 谯公 - Likely a person's name, possibly a historical figure.

    After [榮顯] left, he went to visit Zhang the Attendant's residence and calmly said to him, "Your spirit and bones are clear and refreshing, and your aura and temperament are lofty and distant. However, you have been weighed down by ceremonial robes and hats for thirty to twenty years, living in the mundane world. If you can shed the noisy and vulgar customs and pursue the Dao together, you can achieve immortality within thirty years. My journey here is not driven by mundane concerns but is also to seek out those who cultivate the Dao. I have seen many people, but none like Zhang the Attendant. His words are vague and broad, but he only agrees with them. As I was about to leave, I returned, and my feelings were very sincere. Knowing Zhang's unwillingness, I deeply regretted it. Therefore, I left behind several pills and a sealed envelope containing a yellow document and departed, saying, 'These several pills, if taken, can keep you free of illness. The book records the gains from your official career, and every promotion, you secretly reveal it to yourself. The day the book ends, we will miss each other again.' Later, Zhang the Attendant indeed served in the court, going in and out of the imperial administration, wearing purple and red robes, and inspecting several states. The book records his words in great detail. By the time he was in his thirties, he was the Vice Minister of Revenue. What the book contains is also not much. Although he was prominent in name and position, his teeth and hair had declined. He often spoke about matters related to those who follow the Dao to his friends and relatives. Recalling his spirit, I could not match it." [33]

    华山龙移湫
    　　咸通九年春华阴县南十余里一夕风雷暴作有龙移湫自逺而至先是崖垅高亚无贮水之所此夕从数丈小山从东西直亘南北峯峦草树一无所伤碧波塘湛若疏凿京洛符旅无不枉道而观有好事者自辇毂蒲津相率而至车马不絶逮于累月京城南灵应台有三娘子湫与崖相近水波澄明莫测深浅每秋风揺落未尝有草木飘泛其上或覩片叶纎茎必有飞鸟衔而去之祈祷者多致花钿粉黛及绮罗之啓祝投之歘然而没干符初有朝士数人同游终南山遂及湫所因话灵应之事其间有不信者试以木石投之俄有巨鱼跃出波心鳞甲如雪忽有风雨防晦车马几为暴水所漂迩后人愈敬之莫有敢犯者 [34]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [34]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 咸通九年春 - Refers to the spring of the ninth year of the Xiantong era (868 CE) during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 华阴县南十余里 - Indicates a location about ten li (approximately 5 kilometers) south of Huayin County.
    • 风雷暴作 - Describes a sudden and violent storm with thunder and lightning.
    • 龙移湫 - Literally means 'dragon moves the pond,' which is a supernatural event where a dragon moves a body of water.
    • 从远而至 - Means 'coming from afar.'
    • 崖垅高亚 - Refers to high and low hills and ridges.
    • 贮水之所 - Means 'place to store water.'
    • 碧波荡塘 - Describes a clear, blue, and undulating pond.
    • 湛若疏凿 - Means 'as if it were artificially dug out.'
    • 京洛符旅 - Refers to travelers from the capital (Dongjing or Luoyang).
    • 枉道而观 - Means 'to take a detour to see.'
    • 好事者 - Refers to people who are interested in unusual or supernatural events.
    • 辇毂蒲津 - Refers to the capital (Dongjing or Luoyang) and the city of Puji (Puzhou).
    • 车马不绝 - Means 'continuously crowded with carriages and horses.'
    • 逮于累月 - Means 'for several months.'
    • 灵应台 - Refers to a temple or shrine dedicated to a deity.
    • 三娘子湫 - Refers to a specific pond or spring associated with a deity named San Nangzi.
    • 水波澄明 - Describes clear and transparent water.
    • 莫测深浅 - Means 'impossible to measure the depth.'
    • 秋风摇落 - Refers to the autumn wind causing leaves to fall.
    • 片叶纤茎 - Means 'a small leaf or thin stem.'
    • 飞鸟衔而去之 - Means 'taken away by birds flying away.'
    • 祈祷者 - Refers to people who pray or make offerings.
    • 花钿粉黛 - Refers to cosmetics and decorative items.
    • 绮罗之属 - Refers to fine silk garments.
    • 啓祝投之 - Means 'to offer prayers and throw them in.'
    • 歘然而没 - Means 'suddenly disappear.'
    • 干符初 - Refers to the beginning of the Ganfu era (874-879 CE).
    • 朝士数人 - Refers to several officials.
    • 终南山 - Refers to Mount Zhongnan, a famous mountain range.
    • 灵应之事 - Refers to the miraculous events associated with the deity.
    • 不信者 - Refers to people who do not believe in the supernatural.
    • 木石投之 - Means 'throwing wood and stones into the pond.'
    • 巨鱼跃出波心 - Describes a large fish jumping out of the water.
    • 鳞甲如雪 - Means 'scales like snow.'
    • 风雨防晦 - Refers to a sudden and heavy rainstorm.
    • 暴水 - Means 'flood water.'
    • 几为暴水所漂 - Means 'nearly swept away by the flood.'
    • 迩后 - Refers to a short period of time after the event.
    • 莫有敢犯者 - Means 'nobody dared to violate it.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 咸通九年 (868 CE) is the ninth year of the Xiantong era during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 华阴县 is a county in Shaanxi Province, China.
    • 龙移湫 is a supernatural event involving a dragon moving a body of water, which is a common theme in Chinese folklore and mythology.
    • 京洛 refers to the capital cities of Dongjing (modern-day Kaifeng) and Luoyang, which were important political and cultural centers during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 灵应台 is a temple or shrine dedicated to a deity, often associated with miraculous events.
    • 三娘子湫 is a specific pond or spring associated with a deity named San Nangzi, which is a local folk deity.
    • 干符初 is the beginning of the Ganfu era (874-879 CE), a period during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 终南山 is a famous mountain range in Shaanxi Province, known for its natural beauty and religious significance.

    Proper Nouns

    • 咸通九年 - The ninth year of the Xiantong era (868 CE).
    • 华阴县 - A county in Shaanxi Province, China.
    • 龙移湫 - A supernatural event involving a dragon moving a body of water.
    • 京洛 - The capital cities of Dongjing (modern-day Kaifeng) and Luoyang.
    • 灵应台 - A temple or shrine dedicated to a deity.
    • 三娘子湫 - A specific pond or spring associated with a deity named San Nangzi.
    • 干符初 - The beginning of the Ganfu era (874-879 CE).
    • 终南山 - A famous mountain range in Shaanxi Province.

    In the spring of the ninth year of the Xiantong era (868 CE), a sudden and violent storm with thunder and lightning occurred about ten li south of Huayin County. A dragon moved a body of water from afar, and it came to rest in a place where there were high and low hills and ridges, which was not a suitable location for storing water. The pond was clear and undulating, as if it were artificially dug out. Travelers from the capital (Dongjing or Luoyang) took detours to see this miraculous event. People interested in the event traveled from the capital and other places, and the area was continuously crowded with carriages and horses for several months.

    Near the temple, there was a pond called San Nangzi Pond, which was close to the cliffs. The water was clear and transparent, and its depth was impossible to measure. Every autumn, when the wind caused the leaves to fall, there were never any leaves or stems floating on the surface of the pond. If anyone saw a small leaf or thin stem, it was immediately taken away by birds flying away. People who prayed often offered cosmetics, decorative items, and fine silk garments, which were then thrown into the pond and disappeared suddenly.

    At the beginning of the Ganfu era (874-879 CE), several officials visited Mount Zhongnan and came to the miraculous pond. They discussed the miraculous events associated with the deity. Among them, one official did not believe in the event and threw wood and stones into the pond. Suddenly, a large fish jumped out of the water, with scales like snow. There was also a sudden heavy rainstorm, and the carriages and horses were nearly swept away by the flood. After this event, people became even more reverent, and nobody dared to violate the pond. [34]

    田膨郎偷玉枕
    　　文宗皇帝常持白玉枕徳宗朝于阗国所献追琢奇巧葢希代之寳置于寝殿帐中一旦忽失所在然而禁卫清密非恩泽嫔御莫能至者珍玩罗列他无所失上惊骇移时下诏于都城索贼密谓枢近及左右广中尉曰此非外冦入之为盗者当在禁掖茍求之不获且虞他变一枕诚不足惜卿等卫我皇宫必使罪人斯得不然天子环列自兹无用矣内官惶栗谢罪请以浃旬求捕大悬金帛购求略无寻究之所圣防严切系者渐多坊曲闾巷靡不捜捕有龙武二畨将军王敬常蓄小仆年甫十八九神彩俊利使之无往不届敬曽与流辈于威逺军防宴有侍儿善鼓胡琴四座酒酣因请度曲辞 [35]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [35]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 文宗皇帝 - Refers to Emperor Wenzong of Tang, the ruler of the Tang dynasty.
    • 白玉枕 - A white jade pillow, a precious item.
    • 德宗朝于阗国所献 - Indicates that the pillow was a gift from the kingdom of Yutian during the reign of Emperor Dezong.
    • 追琢奇巧 - Describes the exquisite craftsmanship of the pillow.
    • 希代之寳 - Means 'a treasure of rare times,' emphasizing its uniqueness.
    • 非恩泽嫔御莫能至者 - Indicates that only those favored by the emperor or his concubines could access it.
    • 珍玩罗列 - Means 'treasures and curiosities are arranged,' suggesting a display of valuable items.
    • 他无所失 - Means 'nothing else was lost,' indicating that the disappearance of the pillow was unusual.
    • 惊骇移时 - Means 'shocked and alarmed for a while,' describing the emperor's reaction.
    • 下诏于都城索贼 - Means 'issued an edict in the capital to search for the thief.'
    • 密谓枢近及左右广中尉 - Indicates a secret meeting with the chief eunuchs and other high-ranking officials.
    • 此非外寇入之 - Means 'this is not an external invasion,' suggesting an internal threat.
    • 为盗者当在禁掖 - Means 'the thief must be within the palace grounds.'
    • 苟求之不获 - Means 'if we do not find the thief,' indicating a conditional statement.
    • 且虞他变 - Means 'and fear other changes,' suggesting potential further problems.
    • 一枕诚不足惜 - Means 'one pillow is truly not worth regretting,' indicating a pragmatic view.
    • 卿等卫我皇宫 - Means 'you all guard my palace,' emphasizing the officials' duty.
    • 必使罪人斯得 - Means 'we must surely catch the criminal,' indicating a strong demand.
    • 不然天子环列自兹无用矣 - Means 'otherwise, the circle of the emperor's attendants will be useless from now on,' suggesting a dire consequence.
    • 内官惶栗谢罪 - Means 'the eunuchs were terrified and apologized,' indicating their fear and submission.
    • 请以浃旬求捕 - Means 'they requested to search for ten days,' indicating a specific time frame.
    • 大悬金帛购求 - Means 'they offered a large reward in gold and silk,' indicating a significant incentive.
    • 略无寻究之所 - Means 'there was no trace to be found,' indicating the failure of the search.
    • 圣防严切 - Means 'the emperor's decree was strict,' indicating the severity of the order.
    • 繫者渐多 - Means 'the number of people being detained increased,' indicating the expansion of the investigation.
    • 坊曲闾巷靡不搜捕 - Means 'every street and alley was searched,' indicating a thorough search.
    • 龙武二番将军 - Refers to the commanders of the Dragon and Tiger divisions, elite military units.
    • 王敬弘 - The name of a general.
    • 常蓄小仆 - Means 'usually kept a young servant.'
    • 年甫十八九 - Means 'just eighteen or nineteen years old,' indicating the servant's age.
    • 神彩俊利 - Means 'graceful and agile,' describing the servant's appearance and demeanor.
    • 使之无往不届 - Means 'he sent him everywhere,' indicating the servant's frequent tasks.
    • 敬弘曾与流辈于威远军防宴 - Means 'Wang Jinghong once accompanied his peers to a banquet at the Wei Yuan military camp,' indicating a social event.
    • 有侍儿善鼓胡琴 - Means 'there was a young woman skilled at playing a Huqin (a type of fiddle),' indicating a musical performance.
    • 四座酒酣 - Means 'when everyone was drunk,' indicating the atmosphere of the banquet.
    • 因请度曲辞 - Means 'she then requested to perform a song,' indicating a request for entertainment.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 文宗皇帝 - Emperor Wenzong of Tang (809-846), known for his interest in literature and the arts.
    • 德宗朝 - The reign of Emperor Dezong (780-805), who ruled during the Tang dynasty.
    • 于阗国 - The kingdom of Yutian, located in what is now Xinjiang, China, known for its silk and jade.
    • 追琢奇巧 - Refers to the intricate and beautiful craftsmanship of the pillow.
    • 希代之寳 - Emphasizes the rarity and value of the pillow.
    • 非恩泽嫔御莫能至者 - Indicates the high status and restricted access to the palace.
    • 珍玩罗列 - Suggests a display of valuable items, common in imperial palaces.
    • 他无所失 - Highlights the unusual nature of the loss.
    • 惊骇移时 - Describes the emperor's shock and alarm.
    • 下诏于都城索贼 - Indicates the official response to the theft.
    • 密谓枢近及左右广中尉 - Shows the involvement of high-ranking officials in the investigation.
    • 此非外寇入之 - Suggests an internal threat, reflecting the security concerns of the time.
    • 为盗者当在禁掖 - Indicates the belief that the thief was inside the palace.
    • 苟求之不获 - Expresses the fear of not finding the thief.
    • 且虞他变 - Suggests potential further problems if the thief is not caught.
    • 一枕诚不足惜 - Reflects the pragmatic view of the emperor.
    • 卿等卫我皇宫 - Emphasizes the officials' duty to protect the palace.
    • 必使罪人斯得 - Shows the emperor's demand for the thief to be caught.
    • 不然天子环列自兹无用矣 - Suggests the dire consequences of failing to catch the thief.
    • 内官惶栗谢罪 - Describes the eunuchs' fear and submission.
    • 请以浃旬求捕 - Indicates a specific time frame for the search.
    • 大悬金帛购求 - Shows the significant reward offered.
    • 略无寻究之所 - Indicates the failure of the search.
    • 圣防严切 - Emphasizes the severity of the emperor's decree.
    • 繫者渐多 - Shows the expansion of the investigation.
    • 坊曲闾巷靡不搜捕 - Indicates a thorough search of the city.
    • 龙武二番将军 - Refers to the elite military units.
    • 王敬弘 - A historical figure, a general during the Tang dynasty.
    • 常蓄小仆 - Describes the general's personal servant.
    • 年甫十八九 - Indicates the servant's age.
    • 神彩俊利 - Describes the servant's appearance and demeanor.
    • 使之无往不届 - Indicates the servant's frequent tasks.
    • 敬弘曾与流辈于威远军防宴 - Describes a social event.
    • 有侍儿善鼓胡琴 - Indicates a musical performance.
    • 四座酒酣 - Describes the atmosphere of the banquet.
    • 因请度曲辞 - Indicates a request for entertainment.

    Emperor Wenzong of Tang often kept a white jade pillow, a treasure of rare times, given by the kingdom of Yutian during the reign of Emperor Dezong, in his bedchamber. One day, it suddenly went missing. The palace guards were strict, and only those favored by the emperor or his concubines could access the bedchamber. Other treasures were displayed, but none were lost. The emperor was shocked and alarmed for a while, then issued an edict in the capital to search for the thief. He secretly met with the chief eunuchs and other high-ranking officials and said, "This is not an external invasion; the thief must be within the palace grounds. If we do not find the thief, we fear other changes. One pillow is truly not worth regretting, but you all guard my palace and must surely catch the criminal. Otherwise, the circle of the emperor's attendants will be useless from now on." The eunuchs were terrified and apologized, requesting to search for ten days and offering a large reward in gold and silk. However, there was no trace to be found. The emperor's decree was strict, and the number of people being detained increased. Every street and alley was searched. There was a general of the Dragon and Tiger divisions named Wang Jinghong who usually kept a young servant, just eighteen or nineteen years old, graceful and agile, whom he sent everywhere. Wang Jinghong once accompanied his peers to a banquet at the Wei Yuan military camp, where there was a young woman skilled at playing a Huqin (a type of fiddle). When everyone was drunk, she then requested to perform a song. [35]

    以乐器非妙湏常御手者弹之钟漏已传取之不及因起觧带小仆曰要琵琶顷刻可至敬曰禁鼓才动军门已锁寻常汝岂不见何言之谬也既而就饮数廵小仆以绣囊将琵琶而至座客欢笑曰乐器夲相随所难者惜其妙手南军去左广复三十里入夜且无行伍既而倐忽往来敬惊异如失时又捜捕严意以窃盗疑之宴罢及明遽归其第引而问曰使汝累年不知趫捷如此我闻世有侠客汝莫是否小仆谢曰非有此事但能行尔因言父母俱在蜀中顷年偶至京国今欲却归乡里有一事欲以报恩偷枕者已知姓名三数日当令伏罪敬曰如此即事非等闲因兹令活者不少未知贼 [36]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [36]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 以乐器非妙湏常御手者弹之 - Indicates that the instrument should be played by someone skilled, implying that the usual player is required for it to be played well.
    • 钟漏已传取之不及 - Suggests that the time indicated by the clock had passed, and it was too late to fetch the instrument.
    • 因起觧带 - Means the person got up and loosened his belt, indicating preparation or readiness.
    • 小仆曰要琵琶顷刻可至 - The servant says that the pipa (a type of lute) can be brought in an instant.
    • 禁鼓才动军门已锁 - The guard drums were just beating, and the military gates were already locked.
    • 寻常汝岂不见何言之谬也 - A rhetorical question indicating that the servant should know better and that what he said was incorrect.
    • 既而就饮数廵 - Afterward, they began drinking several rounds.
    • 小仆以绣囊将琵琶而至 - The servant arrived with the pipa in a brocade bag.
    • 座客欢笑曰乐器夲相随所难者惜其妙手 - The guests laughed and said that while instruments usually accompany people, the difficulty lies in finding a skilled player.
    • 南军去左广复三十里 - The southern army was thirty miles away from the left wing.
    • 入夜且无行伍 - At night, there was no formation or order.
    • 既而倐忽往来 - Soon after, there was sudden back-and-forth movement.
    • 敬惊异如失时 - Jing Hong was surprised and lost track of time.
    • 小仆谢曰非有此事但能行尔 - The servant apologized, saying that he had no such thing but could travel quickly.
    • 因言父母俱在蜀中顷年偶至京国今欲却归乡里 - He then explained that his parents were in Sichuan, and he had recently come to the capital and now wanted to return home.
    • 有一事欲以报恩偷枕者已知姓名三数日当令伏罪 - He wanted to repay a debt of gratitude; he knew the name of the thief and would make them confess within a few days.
    • 敬曰如此即事非等闲因兹令活者不少未知贼 - Jing Hong said that such a matter was not trivial and that it had led to many people being saved, but the identity of the thief was still unknown.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 乐器 - Refers to musical instruments, particularly the pipa in this context.
    • 钟漏 - Refers to a timekeeping device, likely a water clock.
    • 禁鼓 - Refers to the drum used to signal the closing of the gates, often associated with military or official settings.
    • 绣囊 - A brocade bag, indicating the high value or care taken with the pipa.
    • 南军 - Refers to the southern army, which could be part of a larger military context.
    • 左广 - Refers to the left wing of an army, indicating a specific location within a military formation.
    • 蜀中 - Refers to Sichuan, a province in southwestern China.
    • 京国 - Refers to the capital city, likely the imperial capital.
    • 偷枕者 - Refers to a thief who stole a pillow, indicating a specific incident of theft.
    • 敬 - Likely a proper name, possibly a noble or official.
    • 小仆 - Refers to a servant, indicating a social hierarchy.

    Proper Nouns

    • 敬 - Likely a proper name, possibly a noble or official.
    • 小仆 - Refers to a servant, indicating a social hierarchy.
    • 蜀中 - Refers to Sichuan, a province in southwestern China.
    • 京国 - Refers to the capital city, likely the imperial capital.
    • 偷枕者 - Refers to a thief who stole a pillow, indicating a specific incident of theft.

    Jing Hong was about to play the pipa, but it was not played well unless it was handled by a skilled player. The clock had already indicated the time, and it was too late to fetch the instrument. He got up and loosened his belt. The servant said, "The pipa can be brought in an instant." Jing Hong replied, "The guard drums were just beating, and the military gates were already locked. You should have known this; what you said is wrong." Afterward, they began drinking several rounds. The servant arrived with the pipa in a brocade bag. The guests laughed and said, "Instruments usually accompany people, but the difficulty lies in finding a skilled player." The southern army was thirty miles away from the left wing, and at night there was no formation or order. Soon after, there was sudden back-and-forth movement. Jing Hong was surprised and lost track of time. The servant apologized, saying that he had no such thing but could travel quickly. He then explained that his parents were in Sichuan, and he had recently come to the capital and now wanted to return home. He wanted to repay a debt of gratitude; he knew the name of the thief and would make them confess within a few days. Jing Hong said, "Such a matter is not trivial and has led to many people being saved, but the identity of the thief is still unknown." [36]

    在何许可报司存掩获否小仆曰偷枕者田膨郎也市军伍行止不恒勇力过人且善超越茍非伺便折其足虽千兵万骑亦将奔走自兹再宿之于望仙门伺便擒之必矣将军随某观之此事仍湏秘密是时渉旬无雨向晓埃尘颇甚车马践人不相见膨郎与少年数軰连臂将入军门小仆执毬杖击之歘然已折左足仰而观之曰我偷枕来不怕他人惟惧于尔既而相值岂复多言于是舁至左军一欵而伏上喜于得贼又知获在禁旅引膨郎临轩诘问具陈常在宫掖往来上曰此乃任侠之流非常窃盗内外囚系数百于是悉令原之小仆初得膨郎已告敬归蜀于是寻之不可但赏敬而已 [37]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [37]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 何可 - Classical expression meaning 'how can' or 'how is it possible,' often used to introduce a question or a statement of impossibility.
    • 报司存掩获否 - Elliptical and ambiguous phrase; could mean 'whether the guard has captured or hidden it' or 'whether the guard has reported capturing or hiding it.'
    • 小仆曰 - '小仆' refers to a minor servant or attendant, and '曰' means 'said.' This phrase introduces a direct speech.
    • 偷枕者田膨郎也 - '偷枕者' means 'the thief of the pillow,' and '田膨郎' is a proper name. The phrase identifies the thief.
    • 市军伍行止不恒勇力过人且善超越 - Describes the thief's characteristics: '市军伍' means 'in the military ranks,' '行止不恒' means 'unpredictable behavior,' '勇力过人' means 'extraordinary bravery and strength,' and '善超越' means 'good at leaping over obstacles.'
    • 茍非伺便折其足虽千兵万骑亦将奔走自兹再宿之于望仙门伺便擒之必矣 - Long sentence describing a plan: '茍非伺便折其足' means 'if not waiting for an opportunity to break his leg,' '虽千兵万骑亦将奔走' means 'even with a thousand soldiers and horsemen, he will flee,' '自兹再宿' means 'from now on for two nights,' '之于望仙门' means 'wait for him at the Gate of the Immortal View,' '伺便擒之必矣' means 'wait for an opportunity to capture him, and it is certain.'
    • 将军随某观之此事仍湏秘密 - '将军' means 'general,' '随某' means 'follow me,' '观之' means 'observe it,' '此事仍湏秘密' means 'this matter still needs to be kept secret.'
    • 是时渉旬无雨向晓埃尘颇甚车马践人不相见 - Describes the weather and the environment: '是时渉旬无雨' means 'at that time, it had been dry for over ten days,' '向晓埃尘颇甚' means 'in the morning, the dust was very thick,' '车马践人不相见' means 'the dust made it impossible to see people even though they were on horseback.'
    • 膨郎与少年数軰连臂将入军门小仆执毬杖击之歘然已折左足仰而观之曰我偷枕来不怕他人惟惧于尔既而相值岂复多言 - Long sentence describing the confrontation: '膨郎与少年数軰连臂将入军门' means 'Penglang and several young men linked arms and were about to enter the military gate,' '小仆执毬杖击之' means 'the minor servant hit him with a ball stick,' '歘然已折左足' means 'suddenly, his left foot was broken,' '仰而观之' means 'he looked up at him,' '曰我偷枕来不怕他人惟惧于尔' means 'he said, 'I stole the pillow, I don't fear others, only you,' '既而相值岂复多言' means 'after that, when they met again, there was no need for more words.'
    • 于是舁至左军一欵而伏 - '于是' means 'therefore,' '舁至左军' means 'carried to the left army,' '一欵而伏' means 'confessed immediately.'
    • 上喜于得贼又知获在禁旅引膨郎临轩诘问具陈常在宫掖往来 - '上喜于得贼' means 'the emperor was pleased to have captured the thief,' '又知获在禁旅' means 'and knew he was captured by the imperial guards,' '引膨郎临轩诘问' means 'brought Penglang to the hall for questioning,' '具陈常在宫掖往来' means 'he confessed that he often came and went in the palace.'
    • 此乃任侠之流非常窃盗内外囚系数百于是悉令原之 - '此乃任侠之流' means 'this is a type of wandering knight,' '非常窃盗' means 'not a common thief,' '内外囚系数百' means 'there were hundreds of prisoners inside and outside,' '于是悉令原之' means 'therefore, all were pardoned.'
    • 小仆初得膨郎已告敬归蜀于是寻之不可但赏敬而已 - '小仆初得膨郎已告敬归蜀' means 'the minor servant initially told Jinghong to return to Shu,' '于是寻之不可' means 'therefore, it was impossible to find him,' '但赏敬而已' means 'only Jinghong was rewarded.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 田膨郎 - A proper name, likely a historical or fictional character.
    • 市军伍 - Refers to the military ranks or service.
    • 望仙门 - A gate, possibly a significant location in the story.
    • 禁旅 - Refers to the imperial guards or elite military units.
    • 宫掖 - Refers to the inner palace or the emperor's residence.
    • 任侠之流 - Refers to wandering knights or chivalrous individuals who often operate outside the law but have a code of honor.

    Proper Nouns

    • 田膨郎 - A proper name, likely a historical or fictional character.
    • 敬 - A proper name, likely a historical or fictional character.
    • 望仙门 - A gate, possibly a significant location in the story.
    • 禁旅 - Refers to the imperial guards or elite military units.
    • 宫掖 - Refers to the inner palace or the emperor's residence.

    How can the thief be reported to the guard? The minor servant said, "The thief of the pillow is Tian Penglang. He serves in the military ranks, his behavior is unpredictable, he has extraordinary bravery and strength, and he is good at leaping over obstacles. If we do not wait for an opportunity to break his leg, even with a thousand soldiers and horsemen, he will flee. From now on for two nights, we should wait for him at the Gate of the Immortal View, and we will certainly capture him when the opportunity arises." The general followed him and observed the situation. This matter still needed to be kept secret. At that time, it had been dry for over ten days, and in the morning, the dust was very thick, making it impossible to see people even though they were on horseback. Penglang and several young men linked arms and were about to enter the military gate. The minor servant hit him with a ball stick, and suddenly, his left foot was broken. Penglang looked up at him and said, "I stole the pillow, I don't fear others, only you." After that, when they met again, there was no need for more words. Therefore, he was carried to the left army and confessed immediately. The emperor was pleased to have captured the thief and knew he was captured by the imperial guards. He brought Penglang to the hall for questioning, and Penglang confessed that he often came and went in the palace. The emperor said, "This is a type of wandering knight, not a common thief. There are hundreds of prisoners inside and outside, so all were pardoned." The minor servant initially told Jinghong to return to Shu, but it was impossible to find him, so only Jinghong was rewarded. [37]

    洛中大水
    　　咸通四年秋洛中大水苑囿庐舍靡不淹没厥后香山寺僧云其日将暮见暴水自龙门川北下有如决江海鼓怒之间殷雷震有二黑牛出于水上掉尾跃空而进众僧与居人慿髙望之谓城中悉为鱼矣俄见定鼎长夏二门隂曀忽开亦有青牛奋跃而出相去约有百歩黑牛奔走而向之怒浪惊澜翕然遂低数丈是夕飘溺尤甚京邑遂至萧条十余年间尚未完葺先是皇城守阍者白昼闻五凤楼中有人歌云天津桥畔火光起魏王堤上看洪水时郑相国涯留守洛师闻之以为妖妄经月余从事宴罢夜归执烛者有火烬遗落骑从才过烟焰已髙救之不及遂烧其半及潦将兴谷洛先涨魏王与月波二堤俱壊乃明阍者之言 [38]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [38]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 咸通四年秋 - In the autumn of the fourth year of Xiantong, where 'Xiantong' is the era name of Emperor Yizong of Tang.
    • 洛中大水 - A great flood in Luoyang, where '洛中' refers to the city of Luoyang.
    • 苑囿庐舍靡不淹没 - The gardens, pavilions, and houses were all submerged, where '靡不' means 'not one but all'.
    • 厥后 - Afterward, used to indicate a time sequence.
    • 香山寺僧云 - The monks of Xiangshan Temple said, where '云' means 'said'.
    • 其日将暮 - As the day was drawing to a close, where '将暮' means 'approaching evening'.
    • 暴水自龙门川北下 - A sudden flood came rushing down from the north of Longmen Creek, where '暴水' means 'sudden flood' and '北下' means 'rushing down from the north'.
    • 有如决江海 - As if the rivers and seas had burst their banks, where '决' means 'burst'.
    • 鼓怒之间 - In the midst of its fury, where '鼓怒' means 'fury'.
    • 殷雷震 - Roaring like thunder, where '殷雷' means 'thunderous sound'.
    • 有二黑牛出于水上 - Two black oxen emerged from the water, where '出于水上' means 'emerged from the water'.
    • 掉尾跃空而进 - Swinging their tails and leaping through the air, where '掉尾' means 'swinging the tail' and '跃空' means 'leaping through the air'.
    • 众僧与居人慿髙望之 - The monks and residents looked down from a high place, where '慿髙望之' means 'looked down from a high place'.
    • 谓城中悉为鱼矣 - They thought the entire city had turned into fish, where '悉为鱼' means 'all turned into fish'.
    • 俄见定鼎长夏二门隂曀忽开 - Soon they saw the two gates of Dinding and Changxia suddenly open, where '隂曀' means 'dark and gloomy' and '忽开' means 'suddenly open'.
    • 亦有青牛奋跃而出 - There was also a blue ox leaping out, where '奋跃' means 'leaping out'.
    • 相去约有百歩 - They were about a hundred steps apart, where '相去' means 'apart' and '约有' means 'about'.
    • 黑牛奔走而 - The black oxen ran away, where '奔走' means 'ran' and '' means 'ran away'.
    • 向之怒浪惊澜翕然遂低数丈 - The previously raging waves and surges suddenly subsided several zhang, where '翕然' means 'suddenly' and '遂低' means 'subsided'.
    • 是夕飘溺尤甚 - That night, the flooding was especially severe, where '飘溺' means 'flooding' and '尤甚' means 'especially severe'.
    • 京邑遂至萧条 - The capital city became desolate, where '遂至' means 'became' and '萧条' means 'desolate'.
    • 十余年间尚未完葺 - For over ten years, it had not been fully repaired, where '尚未完葺' means 'had not been fully repaired'.
    • 先是皇城守阍者白昼闻五凤楼中有人歌云 - Before this, a guard at the imperial city heard someone singing in the Wu Feng Tower during the day, where '白昼' means 'during the day' and '歌云' means 'singing'.
    • 天津桥畔火光起 - Firelight appeared on the banks of the Tianjin Bridge, where '天津桥' is the name of a bridge in Luoyang.
    • 魏王堤上看洪水 - Watching the floodwaters on the Wei Wang Di, where '魏王堤' is the name of a dike in Luoyang.
    • 时郑相国涯留守洛师 - At that time, Chancellor Vang Yai was stationed in Luoyang, where '留守' means 'stationed'.
    • 闻之以为妖妄 - He heard it and thought it was a delusion, where '妖妄' means 'delusion'.
    • 经月余从事宴罢夜归 - After more than a month, the officials returned home after a banquet, where '从事' means 'officials' and '宴罢' means 'after a banquet'.
    • 执烛者有火烬遗落 - The person holding the candle dropped some embers, where '火烬' means 'embers'.
    • 骑从才过烟焰已高 - As soon as the riders passed, the smoke and flames rose high, where '骑从' means 'riders' and '烟焰已高' means 'smoke and flames rose high'.
    • 救之不及遂烧其半 - It was too late to save it, and half of it was burned, where '救之不及' means 'too late to save' and '遂烧其半' means 'half of it was burned'.
    • 及潦将兴谷洛先涨 - When the floodwaters began to rise, the Gu and Luo rivers rose first, where '潦将兴' means 'floodwaters began to rise' and '先涨' means 'rose first'.
    • 魏王与月波二堤俱壊 - The Wei Wang Di and Yuebo Di both broke, where '俱壊' means 'both broke'.
    • 乃明阍者之言 - Thus, the guard's words were proven true, where '乃明' means 'thus proved true' and '阍者之言' means 'the guard's words'.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 咸通四年 - The fourth year of the Xiantong era (863 CE) during the reign of Emperor Yizong of Tang.
    • 洛中 - Refers to the city of Luoyang, an important city in Chinese history and the eastern capital of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 香山寺 - Xiangshan Temple, a famous temple in Luoyang.
    • 龙门川 - Longmen Creek, a location near the Longmen Grottoes.
    • 定鼎长夏二门 - The two gates of Dinding and Changxia, likely referring to specific locations in Luoyang.
    • 天津桥 - Tianjin Bridge, a historical bridge in Luoyang.
    • 魏王堤 - Wei Wang Di, a dike in Luoyang.
    • 月波二堤 - Yuebo Di, another dike in Luoyang.
    • 郑相国涯 - Chancellor Vang Yai, a historical figure during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 五凤楼 - Wu Feng Tower, a building in the imperial city.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • This long sequence must be read as a single statement describing the extent of the flooding.
    • This long, complex sentence must be broken down into logical segments to maintain clarity.
    • This sequence describes a series of events and must be read in a logical order.
    • This sequence describes the aftermath of the flood and must be read as a coherent narrative.
    • This sequence describes a series of events leading up to the flood and must be read in a logical order.
    • This sequence describes a specific incident and must be read in a logical order.
    • This sequence describes the final events and must be read in a logical order.

    Proper Nouns

    • 咸通四年 - The fourth year of the Xiantong era (863 CE).
    • 洛中 - The city of Luoyang.
    • 香山寺 - Xiangshan Temple.
    • 龙门川 - Longmen Creek.
    • 定鼎长夏二门 - The two gates of Dinding and Changxia.
    • 天津桥 - Tianjin Bridge.
    • 魏王堤 - Wei Wang Di.
    • 月波二堤 - Yuebo Di.
    • 郑相国涯 - Chancellor Vang Yai.
    • 五凤楼 - Wu Feng Tower.

    In the autumn of the fourth year of Xiantong, a great flood struck Luoyang, submerging all the gardens, pavilions, and houses. Afterward, the monks of Xiangshan Temple reported that as the day was drawing to a close, they saw a sudden flood rushing down from the north of Longmen Creek, as if the rivers and seas had burst their banks. In the midst of its fury, it roared like thunder. Two black oxen emerged from the water, swinging their tails and leaping through the air. The monks and residents looked down from a high place and thought the entire city had turned into fish. Soon they saw the two gates of Dinding and Changxia suddenly open, and there was also a blue ox leaping out. They were about a hundred steps apart. The black oxen ran away, and the previously raging waves and surges suddenly subsided several zhang. That night, the flooding was especially severe, and the capital city became desolate. For over ten years, it had not been fully repaired.

    Before this, a guard at the imperial city heard someone singing in the Wu Feng Tower during the day: "Firelight appeared on the banks of the Tianjin Bridge; watching the floodwaters on the Wei Wang Di." At that time, Chancellor Vang Yai was stationed in Luoyang, and he heard it and thought it was a delusion. After more than a month, the officials returned home after a banquet. The person holding the candle dropped some embers, and as soon as the riders passed, the smoke and flames rose high. It was too late to save it, and half of it was burned. When the floodwaters began to rise, the Gu and Luo rivers rose first, and the Wei Wang Di and Yuebo Di both broke. Thus, the guard's words were proven true. [38]

    李朱崖知白令公
    　　白中书方居郎署未有知者唯朱崖李相国器之许于搢绅间多所延誉然而资用不充无以祗奉僚友一旦相国遗钱十万俾为酒肴之备约省阁名士数人尅日同过其第时秋暮隂沈渉旬霖沥贺拔惎员外求官未遂将欲出京薄游与白公同年登第羸驹就门告别阍者以方俟朝客乃以他适对之贺拔惎遂驻车留书备述羁游之意白公览书叹曰丈夫处世穷逹当有时命茍不才以侥幸取容未足为发身之道岂家蓄美止邀当路豪贵曩时登第贫交今日闭关不接纵使便无荣显又安得不愧于懐遽令仆者命贺拔惎囘车遂以杯盘同费俄而所约朝贤聮骑而至阍者具陈贺 [39]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [39]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 李朱崖知白令公 - A compound title for Li Zhuyi, a high-ranking official. '知白' is a personal name, and '令公' is a respectful title.
    • 中书方居郎署未有知者 - '中书' refers to the position of a central government official, '郎署' is a department or office, and '未有知者' means 'no one knows.'
    • 朱崖李相国器之许于搢绅间多所延誉 - '朱崖' is a place name, '相国' is a high-ranking official, '器之' means 'to regard with favor,' '搢绅' refers to the elite or scholars, and '延誉' means 'to spread one's reputation.'
    • 资用不充无以祗奉僚友 - '资用' means 'resources or funds,' '不充' means 'insufficient,' '无以' means 'unable to,' and '祗奉' means 'to serve or support.'
    • 一旦相国遗钱十万俾为酒肴之备 - '一旦' means 'one day,' '遗钱' means 'left money,' '十万' is 'ten thousand,' '俾' means 'to make or have,' and '酒肴之备' means 'preparation of food and drink.'
    • 约省阁名士数人尅日同过其第 - '约' means 'to invite,' '省阁' refers to high-ranking officials, '名士' means 'famous scholars,' '尅日' means 'on a specific day,' and '同过其第' means 'to visit his residence together.'
    • 时秋暮隂沈渉旬霖沥 - '时' means 'at the time,' '秋暮' means 'late autumn,' '隂沈' means 'gloomy,' '渉旬' means 'over ten days,' and '霖沥' means 'continuous rain.'
    • 贺拔惎员外求官未遂将欲出京薄游 - '贺拔惎' is a personal name, '员外' is an official title, '求官未遂' means 'failed to obtain an official position,' '将欲' means 'intends to,' '出京' means 'leave the capital,' and '薄游' means 'a brief journey.'
    • 与白公同年登第羸驹就门告别 - '同年登第' means 'passed the imperial examination in the same year,' '羸驹' means 'a weak horse,' '就门' means 'at the gate,' and '告别' means 'to say goodbye.'
    • 阍者以方俟朝客乃以他适对之 - '阍者' means 'gatekeeper,' '方俟' means 'is waiting,' '朝客' means 'officials,' and '他适' means 'elsewhere.'
    • 贺拔惎遂驻车留书备述羁游之意 - '遂' means 'then,' '驻车' means 'stop the carriage,' '留书' means 'leave a letter,' '备述' means 'fully describe,' and '羁游之意' means 'the intention of being on a journey.'
    • 白公览书叹曰 - '览书' means 'read the letter,' and '叹曰' means 'exclaimed.'
    • 丈夫处世穷达当有时命 - '丈夫' means 'man,' '处世' means 'in life,' '穷达' means 'poverty or wealth,' and '有时命' means 'is determined by fate.'
    • 苟不才以侥幸取容未足为发身之道 - '苟' means 'if,' '不才' means 'unworthy,' '侥幸' means 'by chance,' '取容' means 'to gain favor,' and '未足为发身之道' means 'is not a proper way to advance.'
    • 岂家蓄美止邀当路豪贵 - '岂' means 'how can,' '家蓄' means 'keep at home,' '美' means 'beautiful things,' '止邀' means 'only invite,' and '当路豪贵' means 'powerful officials.'
    • 曩时登第贫交今日闭关不接 - '曩时' means 'in the past,' '登第' means 'passed the imperial examination,' '贫交' means 'poor friends,' '今日' means 'today,' '闭关' means 'shut off,' and '不接' means 'not to meet.'
    • 纵使便无荣显又安得不愧于怀 - '纵使' means 'even if,' '便' means 'then,' '无荣显' means 'no honor or fame,' '又安得' means 'how can,' and '不愧于怀' means 'not feel ashamed.'
    • 遽令仆者命贺拔惎囘车遂以杯盘同费 - '遽令' means 'immediately ordered,' '仆者' means 'servant,' '命' means 'to order,' '囘车' means 'to turn back the carriage,' and '遂以杯盘同费' means 'then shared the food and drink.'
    • 俄而所约朝贤聮骑而至阍者具陈贺 - '俄而' means 'soon,' '所约' means 'the ones invited,' '朝贤' means 'officials,' '聮骑' means 'riding together,' '而至' means 'arrived,' '阍者' means 'gatekeeper,' '具陈' means 'reported in detail,' and '贺' means 'congratulated.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 李朱崖知白令公 - Refers to Li Zhuyi, a high-ranking official.
    • 中书方居郎署未有知者 - Refers to the central government office where Li Zhuyi worked.
    • 朱崖李相国器之许于搢绅间多所延誉 - Refers to the practice of spreading one's reputation among scholars and officials.
    • 资用不充无以祗奉僚友 - Refers to the financial difficulties faced by officials.
    • 一旦相国遗钱十万俾为酒肴之备 - Refers to the tradition of officials hosting gatherings for scholars.
    • 约省阁名士数人尅日同过其第 - Refers to the practice of high-ranking officials inviting scholars to their residences.
    • 时秋暮隂沈渉旬霖沥 - Refers to the weather conditions in late autumn.
    • 贺拔惎员外求官未遂将欲出京薄游 - Refers to the practice of officials traveling briefly when they fail to obtain a position.
    • 与白公同年登第羸驹就门告别 - Refers to the tradition of scholars passing the imperial examination together.
    • 阍者以方俟朝客乃以他适对之 - Refers to the role of gatekeepers in official residences.
    • 贺拔惎遂驻车留书备述羁游之意 - Refers to the practice of leaving letters during travels.
    • 白公览书叹曰 - Refers to the emotional response of officials to letters.
    • 丈夫处世穷达当有时命 - Refers to the philosophical view of fate in Chinese culture.
    • 苟不才以侥幸取容未足为发身之道 - Refers to the moral view of advancement through merit.
    • 岂家蓄美止邀当路豪贵 - Refers to the practice of officials entertaining powerful officials.
    • 曩时登第贫交今日闭关不接 - Refers to the social dynamics among scholars.
    • 纵使便无荣显又安得不愧于怀 - Refers to the moral feelings of officials.
    • 遽令仆者命贺拔惎囘车遂以杯盘同费 - Refers to the practice of sharing food and drink during gatherings.
    • 俄而所约朝贤聮骑而至阍者具陈贺 - Refers to the arrival of officials and the role of gatekeepers.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • 李朱崖知白令公 - The title '李朱崖知白令公' is a compound title that needs to be rendered carefully.
    • 中书方居郎署未有知者 - The sentence structure is straightforward but requires careful translation to maintain the meaning.
    • 朱崖李相国器之许于搢绅间多所延誉 - The sentence is a bit complex, with multiple clauses that need to be translated clearly.
    • 资用不充无以祗奉僚友 - The sentence structure is simple but requires careful translation to maintain the meaning.
    • 一旦相国遗钱十万俾为酒肴之备 - The sentence structure is straightforward but requires careful translation to maintain the meaning.
    • 约省阁名士数人尅日同过其第 - The sentence structure is straightforward but requires careful translation to maintain the meaning.
    • 时秋暮隂沈渉旬霖沥 - The sentence structure is straightforward but requires careful translation to maintain the meaning.
    • 贺拔惎员外求官未遂将欲出京薄游 - The sentence structure is straightforward but requires careful translation to maintain the meaning.

    Li Zhuyi, the Righteous and Wise Duke,

    Li Bai, the Middle Secretary, was stationed in the Langshu office, and no one knew him. Only the Righteous and Wise Duke of Zhuji admired him and spread his reputation among the scholars. However, his resources were insufficient to support his colleagues. One day, the Duke left him ten thousand coins to prepare for food and drink, and invited several distinguished scholars from the Shengge to visit his residence on a specific day. It was late autumn, and the weather had been gloomy and rainy for over ten days. Heba Ji, an official outside the capital, had failed to obtain a position and intended to take a brief journey. He had passed the imperial examination in the same year as Li Bai and was about to leave when he arrived at the gate to say goodbye. The gatekeeper, who was waiting for officials, told him that they were elsewhere. Heba Ji then stopped his carriage and left a letter, fully describing his intention of being on a journey. Li Bai read the letter and exclaimed, "A man in life, his poverty or wealth is determined by fate. If he is unworthy and gains favor by chance, it is not a proper way to advance. How can he keep beautiful things at home only to invite powerful officials? In the past, when we passed the imperial examination together, we were poor friends, but today, he has closed his door and does not meet. Even if he has no honor or fame, how can he not feel ashamed? I immediately ordered the servant to have Heba Ji turn back his carriage, and then we shared the food and drink. Soon, the invited officials arrived, riding together, and the gatekeeper reported in detail. [39]

    抜惎从容无不惋愕而去翌日于私第谒见相国询朝士来者为谁白公对以賔客未至适有同年出京访别悯其龙钟委困不忍弃之留饮数杯遂阙祗接既负吹嘘之际甘从谴斥之罪相国称赏逾时云此事真古人之道由兹贵达所以激劝浇薄不旬月自使下评事先授美官白公以库部郎中入为翰林学士未逾三载便秉钧衡其后五镇藩维再居廊庙蹈义懐仁而终始一致流芳传素士林美之大中初边鄙不寜土蕃尤甚恣其倔强宣宗欲致讨伐遂于延英殿先问宰臣公首奏兴师请为统帅防边藩镇兵士数万鼓行而前时犬戎临阵平川以生骑数千伏藏山谷既而得于谍者遂设 [40]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [40]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 从容无不惋愕而去 - A phrase meaning 'left with a composed yet regretful expression.'
    • 蹈义怀仁而终始一致 - A phrase meaning 'upheld righteousness and benevolence consistently throughout his career.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 相国 - Refers to the Chancellor, a high-ranking official in the imperial government.
    • 朝士 - Refers to officials or bureaucrats.
    • 同年 - Refers to colleagues or fellow officials, often used in the context of those who passed the same imperial examinations.
    • 库部郎中 - A specific position in the treasury department.
    • 翰林学士 - A scholar at the Hanlin Academy, a prestigious institution that advised the emperor.
    • 大中 - The reign title of Emperor Xuānzōng (唐宣宗) of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 延英殿 - A palace hall where the emperor would consult with his ministers.
    • 犬戎 - An ancient term referring to the Tibetans or other nomadic tribes.
    • 土蕃 - Another term for the Tibetans.

    Proper Nouns

    • 抜惎 - Likely a name or title, but its exact meaning is unclear.
    • 相国 - The Chancellor.
    • 朝士 - Officials or bureaucrats.
    • 同年 - Colleagues or fellow officials.
    • 库部郎中 - A position in the treasury department.
    • 翰林学士 - A scholar at the Hanlin Academy.
    • 大中 - The reign title of Emperor Xuānzōng (唐宣宗) of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 延英殿 - A palace hall where the emperor would consult with his ministers.
    • 犬戎 - An ancient term referring to the Tibetans or other nomadic tribes.
    • 土蕃 - Another term for the Tibetans.

    抜惎从容无不惋愕而去。翌日，于私第谒见相国，询朝士来者为谁。白公对以宾客未至，适有同年出京访别，悯其龙钟委困，不忍弃之，留饮数杯，遂阙祗接。既负吹嘘之际，甘从谴斥之罪。相国称赏逾时，云此事真古人之道，由兹贵达，所以激劝浇薄。不旬月，自使下评事，先授美官。白公以库部郎中入为翰林学士，未逾三载，便秉钧衡。其后五镇藩维，再居廊庙，蹈义怀仁，而终始一致，流芳传素，士林美之。大中初，边鄙不宁，土蕃尤甚，恣其倔强。宣宗欲致讨伐，遂于延英殿先问宰臣，公首奏兴师，请为统帅，防边藩镇兵士数万，鼓行而前。时犬戎临阵平川，以生骑数千伏藏山谷，既而得于谍者，遂设。 [40]

    奇兵待之有蕃中首帅衣绯茸裘系宝装带所乗白马骏异无比锋镝未交鞭出于阵面者数四频召汉军鬭将白公诫兵士无得应之俄而驻军指挥背我师百余歩而立有潞州小将骁勇善射请快马弯弧而出连发两矢皆中其项跃马而前抽短劒踣于鞍上以手扶挟如鬬敌之状蕃将士卒但呼噪助之于是脱绯裘觧金带夺马而还师旅无不奋勇既大战沙漠虏陈瓦解土崩乗胜追奔几及黑山之下所获駞马辎重不可胜计束手而降三四千人先是河湟关郡界内在匈奴自此悉为内地宣皇初览防书云我知敏中必殄凶丑白公凯旋与同列宰相进诗云一诏皇城四海颁丑戎 [41]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [41]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 奇兵待之 - Military strategy meaning 'to await the enemy with an unexpected force,' implying a surprise attack.
    • 锋镝未交 - Military term meaning 'before the weapons have clashed,' indicating the early stage of battle.
    • 扬鞭出于阵面者数四 - Describes aggressive actions: 'he whipped his horse out onto the battlefield multiple times.'
    • 频召汉军斗将 - Means 'he repeatedly challenged the Han army's generals to fight.'
    • 白公诫兵士无得应之 - Means 'General Bai warned his soldiers not to respond.'
    • 俄而驻军指挥背我师百余步而立 - Means 'soon, he halted his troops and stood facing us, about a hundred steps away.'
    • 有潞州小将骁勇善射 - Describes a brave and skilled archer from the state of Lu.
    • 请快马弯弧而出 - Means 'he requested a fast horse and drew his bow, riding out.'
    • 连发两矢皆中其项 - Means 'he fired two arrows in succession, both hitting the leader's neck.'
    • 跃马而前抽短剑踣于鞍上 - Means 'he leaped forward on his horse, drew his short sword, and fell to the ground on the saddle.'
    • 以手扶挟如斗敌之状 - Means 'he held himself up with his hands as if engaged in battle.'
    • 但呼噪助之 - Means 'the soldiers only shouted and cheered him on.'
    • 于是脱绯裘解金带夺马而还 - Means 'he then removed his red fur coat, took off his golden belt, and rode back on the enemy's horse.'
    • 师旅无不奋勇 - Means 'the entire army was inspired to fight bravely.'
    • 既大战沙漠虏陈瓦解土崩 - Means 'after a great battle in the desert, the enemy's formation collapsed like a landslide.'
    • 乘胜追奔几及黑山之下 - Means 'we pursued the fleeing enemy almost to the foot of Heishan Mountain.'
    • 所获驼马辎重不可胜计 - Means 'the spoils of war, including camels, horses, and supplies, were too numerous to count.'
    • 束手而降三四千人 - Means 'about three or four thousand people surrendered.'
    • 宣皇初览防书云 - Means 'when Emperor Xuan first read the report, he said,'
    • 我知敏中必殄凶丑 - Means 'I knew that Mingzhong would surely destroy the wicked.'
    • 白公凯旋与同列宰相进诗云 - Means 'when General Bai returned victorious, he and the other chancellors presented him with a poem saying,'
    • 一诏皇城四海颁 - Means 'one edict from the imperial capital was sent throughout the empire.'
    • 丑戎 - Refers to the enemy or foreign forces.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 蕃中首帅 - Likely refers to a leader from a nomadic or foreign tribe, possibly the Xiongnu or another northern ethnic group.
    • 绯茸裘 - A luxurious fur coat, indicating the leader's high status.
    • 宝装带 - A belt adorned with precious ornaments, further emphasizing the leader's wealth and rank.
    • 白马骏异无比 - A white horse was often a symbol of nobility and strength in Chinese culture.
    • 白公 - Likely refers to a Han general named Bai, though the specific identity is unclear without further context.
    • 潞州小将 - Refers to a brave and skilled archer from the state of Lu, possibly a reference to the Tang Dynasty.
    • 河湟关郡 - Refers to the Hexi and Guanzhong regions, important areas in Chinese history.
    • 宣皇 - Likely refers to Emperor Xuan of the Tang Dynasty, known for his military campaigns against the Xiongnu.
    • 敏中 - Likely refers to a general or military leader named Mingzhong, possibly a reference to the Tang Dynasty general Guo Ziyi.
    • 黑山 - Heishan Mountain, a geographical location in Chinese history.

    Proper Nouns

    • 蕃中首帅 - A high-ranking leader from a foreign tribe or region.
    • 白公 - Likely refers to a Han general named Bai.
    • 潞州小将 - A brave and skilled archer from the state of Lu.
    • 河湟关郡 - The Hexi and Guanzhong regions.
    • 宣皇 - Likely refers to Emperor Xuan of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 敏中 - Likely refers to a general or military leader named Mingzhong.
    • 黑山 - Heishan Mountain, a geographical location in Chinese history.
    • 丑戎 - Refers to the enemy or foreign forces.

    Awaiting the enemy with an unexpected force, a high-ranking leader from a foreign tribe appeared, wearing a red fur coat and a belt adorned with precious ornaments, riding an exceptionally swift and rare white steed. Before the weapons had clashed, he whipped his horse out onto the battlefield multiple times, repeatedly challenging the Han army's generals to fight. General Bai warned his soldiers not to respond. Soon, he halted his troops and stood facing us, about a hundred steps away. A brave and skilled archer from the state of Lu requested a fast horse and drew his bow, riding out. He fired two arrows in succession, both hitting the leader's neck. He leaped forward on his horse, drew his short sword, and fell to the ground on the saddle, holding himself up with his hands as if engaged in battle. The soldiers only shouted and cheered him on. He then removed his red fur coat, took off his golden belt, and rode back on the enemy's horse. The entire army was inspired to fight bravely. After a great battle in the desert, the enemy's formation collapsed like a landslide. We pursued the fleeing enemy almost to the foot of Heishan Mountain. The spoils of war, including camels, horses, and supplies, were too numerous to count. About three or four thousand people surrendered. Prior to this, the regions of Hexi and Guanzhong were under the control of the Xiongnu, but from now on they became part of the interior. When Emperor Xuan first read the report, he said, "I knew that Mingzhong would surely destroy the wicked." When General Bai returned victorious, he and the other chancellors presented him with a poem saying, "One edict from the imperial capital was sent throughout the empire." The enemy forces were vanquished. [41]

    无数束身还戍楼吹笛人休战牧野嘶风马遽闲河水九盘数曲陇山千里锁诸关西边北塞今无事为报东南夷与蛮马相植诗云舜德尧仁化犬戎许提河陇欵皇风指挥武皆神筭开拓乾坤是圣功四帅有征无汗马七关虽戍已弢弓天留此事还英主不在他年在大中魏相扶诗云萧关新复万山川古戍秦原象纬鲜戎虏乞降归惠化皇威渐慑腥羶穹庐逺戍烟尘灭神武光竹帛传左袵尽知歌帝泽从兹不更备三边崔相诗云邉陲万里注恩波宇宙羣方洽凯歌右地名王争解辫逺方戎垒尽投戈烟尘永息三秋戍瑞气遥清九折河共偶圣明千载运更观俗阜与时和 [42]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [42]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 无数束身还戍楼 - Compound phrase referring to soldiers returning to their watchtowers, possibly with a sense of duty or obligation.
    • 吹笛人休战 - Metaphorical expression where 'blowing the flute' symbolizes a call to peace or a cessation of conflict.
    • 牧野嘶风马遽闲 - Describes a scene where horses in the wilderness neigh in the wind, suggesting a moment of calm after battle.
    • 河水九盘数曲陇山千里锁诸关 - Vivid description of the winding river and the mountains that guard the passes, emphasizing the strategic importance of the terrain.
    • 西边北塞今无事 - Indicates that the western and northern borders are currently peaceful.
    • 为报东南夷与蛮 - Suggests a report or message about the eastern and southern regions, possibly indicating that the frontier tribes have submitted.
    • 马相植诗云舜德尧仁化犬戎许提河陇款皇风 - Reference to ancient poetry praising the virtues of emperors like Shun and Yao, and their influence on distant peoples.
    • 指挥武皆神筭开拓乾坤是圣功 - Describes military strategy as divine calculation and the expansion of territory as a great achievement of a sage ruler.
    • 四帅有征无汗马 - Indicates that the four generals have been successful in their campaigns without the need for heavy fighting.
    • 七关虽戍已弢弓 - Suggests that the seven passes, though still guarded, have seen a reduction in military tension.
    • 天留此事还英主 - Implies that the current state of peace and prosperity is due to the wisdom and leadership of a great ruler.
    • 不在他年在大中魏相扶 - Suggests that the stability and success are due to the support of a wise minister like Wei Zheng.
    • 萧关新复万山川古戍秦原象纬鲜 - Describes the recent recovery of the Xiangguan region, with its ancient fortifications and clear skies.
    • 戎虏乞降归惠化皇威渐慑腥羶 - Indicates that the enemy has surrendered, influenced by the benevolent rule and growing power of the emperor.
    • 穹庐远戍烟尘灭神武光竹帛传 - Describes the peace and prosperity spreading far and wide, with the emperor's martial prowess celebrated in history.
    • 左袵尽知歌帝泽从兹不更备三边 - Suggests that the people on the eastern frontier know the emperor's benevolence and no longer need to prepare for defense.
    • 崔相诗云边陲万里注恩波宇宙群方洽凯歌右地名王争解辫远方戎垒尽投戈烟尘永息三秋戍瑞气遥清九折河共偶圣明千载运更观俗阜与时和 - A longer poetic passage that continues to praise the emperor's rule and the resulting peace and prosperity.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 无数束身还戍楼 - Refers to soldiers returning to their watchtowers, a common theme in classical Chinese literature.
    • 吹笛人休战 - Symbolizes peace and the cessation of conflict, often used in classical poetry.
    • 牧野嘶风马遽闲 - Refers to the scene of horses in the wilderness, a common imagery in classical Chinese literature.
    • 河水九盘数曲陇山千里锁诸关 - Describes the strategic importance of the terrain, often mentioned in historical accounts of border defense.
    • 西边北塞今无事 - Indicates the current peace on the western and northern borders, a common theme in imperial court poetry.
    • 为报东南夷与蛮 - Refers to the submission of the eastern and southern frontier tribes, a common theme in imperial court poetry.
    • 马相植诗云舜德尧仁化犬戎许提河陇款皇风 - References the virtues of ancient emperors like Shun and Yao, and their influence on distant peoples, a common theme in classical Chinese literature.
    • 指挥武皆神筭开拓乾坤是圣功 - Describes military strategy as divine calculation and the expansion of territory as a great achievement of a sage ruler, a common theme in imperial court poetry.
    • 四帅有征无汗马 - Indicates the success of the four generals in their campaigns without heavy fighting, a common theme in military chronicles.
    • 七关虽戍已弢弓 - Suggests the reduction in military tension at the seven passes, a common theme in imperial court poetry.
    • 天留此事还英主 - Implies the current state of peace and prosperity is due to the wisdom and leadership of a great ruler, a common theme in imperial court poetry.
    • 不在他年在大中魏相扶 - Suggests the stability and success are due to the support of a wise minister like Wei Zheng, a historical reference.
    • 萧关新复万山川古戍秦原象纬鲜 - Describes the recent recovery of the Xiangguan region, a historical reference.
    • 戎虏乞降归惠化皇威渐慑腥羶 - Indicates the surrender of the enemy due to the benevolent rule and growing power of the emperor, a common theme in imperial court poetry.
    • 穹庐远戍烟尘灭神武光竹帛传 - Describes the peace and prosperity spreading far and wide, a common theme in imperial court poetry.
    • 左袵尽知歌帝泽从兹不更备三边 - Suggests the people on the eastern frontier know the emperor's benevolence and no longer need to prepare for defense, a common theme in imperial court poetry.
    • 崔相诗云边陲万里注恩波宇宙群方洽凯歌右地名王争解辫远方戎垒尽投戈烟尘永息三秋戍瑞气遥清九折河共偶圣明千载运更观俗阜与时和 - A longer poetic passage that continues to praise the emperor's rule and the resulting peace and prosperity, a common theme in imperial court poetry.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • 无数束身还戍楼吹笛人休战牧野嘶风马遽闲河水九盘数曲陇山千里锁诸关西边北塞今无事为报东南夷与蛮 - This long sequence must be read as a series of descriptive phrases that paint a picture of peace and prosperity.
    • 马相植诗云舜德尧仁化犬戎许提河陇款皇风指挥武皆神筭开拓乾坤是圣功四帅有征无汗马七关虽戍已弢弓天留此事还英主不在他年在大中魏相扶 - This sequence describes the emperor's virtues and the success of his generals, with a focus on the support of wise ministers.
    • 萧关新复万山川古戍秦原象纬鲜戎虏乞降归惠化皇威渐慑腥羶穹庐远戍烟尘灭神武光竹帛传左袵尽知歌帝泽从兹不更备三边 - This sequence continues the theme of peace and prosperity, with a focus on the submission of the enemy and the celebration of the emperor's rule.
    • 崔相诗云边陲万里注恩波宇宙群方洽凯歌右地名王争解辫远方戎垒尽投戈烟尘永息三秋戍瑞气遥清九折河共偶圣明千载运更观俗阜与时和 - This longer poetic passage further emphasizes the peace and prosperity brought by the emperor's rule.

    Proper Nouns

    • 无数束身还戍楼 - No proper nouns.
    • 吹笛人休战 - No proper nouns.
    • 牧野嘶风马遽闲 - No proper nouns.
    • 河水九盘数曲陇山千里锁诸关 - No proper nouns.
    • 西边北塞今无事 - No proper nouns.
    • 为报东南夷与蛮 - No proper nouns.
    • 马相植诗云舜德尧仁化犬戎许提河陇款皇风 - No proper nouns.
    • 指挥武皆神筭开拓乾坤是圣功 - No proper nouns.
    • 四帅有征无汗马 - No proper nouns.
    • 七关虽戍已弢弓 - No proper nouns.
    • 天留此事还英主 - No proper nouns.
    • 不在他年在大中魏相扶 - No proper nouns.
    • 萧关新复万山川古戍秦原象纬鲜 - No proper nouns.
    • 戎虏乞降归惠化皇威渐慑腥羶 - No proper nouns.
    • 穹庐远戍烟尘灭神武光竹帛传 - No proper nouns.
    • 左袵尽知歌帝泽从兹不更备三边 - No proper nouns.
    • 崔相诗云边陲万里注恩波宇宙群方洽凯歌右地名王争解辫远方戎垒尽投戈烟尘永息三秋戍瑞气遥清九折河共偶圣明千载运更观俗阜与时和 - No proper nouns.

    A Poem of Peace and Prosperity

    Soldiers return to their watchtowers, the flutes signaling a cessation of war. In the wilderness, horses neigh in the wind, and the river winds through the mountains, locking the passes for a thousand miles. The western and northern borders are now peaceful, and news is brought from the eastern and southern regions of the submission of the frontier tribes. Ancient poetry speaks of the virtues of Emperor Shun and Emperor Yao, who tamed the distant peoples of the Dog and Qiang tribes. The emperor's benevolent rule and martial prowess have been celebrated, and the expansion of the empire is seen as a great achievement of a sage ruler. The four generals have been successful in their campaigns without heavy fighting, and the seven passes, though still guarded, have seen a reduction in military tension. The current state of peace and prosperity is due to the wisdom and leadership of a great ruler, supported by the counsel of wise ministers like Wei Zheng. The recent recovery of the Xiangguan region has been marked by clear skies and the submission of the enemy, influenced by the emperor's benevolent rule and growing power. The peace and prosperity have spread far and wide, with the emperor's martial prowess celebrated in history. The people on the eastern frontier know the emperor's benevolence and no longer need to prepare for defense. The poem continues to praise the emperor's rule and the resulting peace and prosperity, emphasizing the submission of the enemy, the celebration of the emperor's rule, and the harmony of the times. [42]

    剧谈录卷上
    　　钦定四库全书
    　　剧谈录卷下　　　　　　　唐　康骈　撰刘相国宅
    　　通义坊刘相国宅本文宗朝朔方节度使李进贤旧第进贤起自戎旅而倜傥瑰玮累居藩翰富于财寳虽豪侈奉身雅好賔客有中朝宿德常话在名塲日失意边游进贤接纳甚至其后京华相遇时亦造其门属牡丹盛开因以赏花为名及期而往防事备陈饮馔宴席之间已非寻常举杯数廵复引众賔归内室宇华丽楹柱皆设锦绣列筵甚广器用悉是黄金堦前有花数丛覆以锦幄妓妾俱服纨绮执丝簧善歌舞者至多客之左右皆有女仆双鬟者二人所须无不必至承接之意常日指使者不如芳酒绮肴穷极水陆至于仆乗供给靡不丰盈自午迄于眀晨不覩杯盘狼籍朝士云迩后歴观豪 [43]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [43]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 剧谈录 - A title for a collection of anecdotes or conversations, often used in classical Chinese literature.
    • 钦定四库全书 - Authorized by the Emperor, the Four Treasuries of Books, referring to a monumental collection of Chinese literature commissioned during the Qing dynasty.
    • 通义坊刘相国宅 - The residence of the Duke of Tongyi in Tongyi Lane, indicating a specific location and the status of the owner.
    • 本文宗朝朔方节度使李进贤旧第 - The former residence of Li Jinxian, the military governor of Shaofang during the reign of Emperor Wenzong.
    • 起自戎旅而倜傥瑰玮 - Rising from military service, he was distinguished and remarkable.
    • 累居藩翰 - Having held several positions as a frontier governor.
    • 富于财寳 - Wealthy in wealth and treasures.
    • 豪侈奉身 - Living a luxurious and extravagant life.
    • 雅好賔客 - Having a refined taste for entertaining guests.
    • 中朝宿德 - Eminent scholars and officials of the imperial court.
    • 失意边游 - Having experienced setbacks while traveling on the borders.
    • 接纳甚至 - He welcomed them with great enthusiasm.
    • 京华相遇时亦造其门 - When they met in the capital, they also visited his residence.
    • 牡丹盛开 - The peony flowers were in full bloom.
    • 赏花为名 - Celebrating the peony flowers.
    • 及期而往 - On the appointed day, they went there.
    • 防事备陈 - Preparations were made in advance.
    • 饮馔宴席之间已非寻常 - The food and drink at the banquet were not ordinary.
    • 举杯数廵 - After several rounds of toasting.
    • 引众賔归内室 - He led the guests into the inner chambers.
    • 宇华丽楹柱皆设锦绣 - The rooms were magnificent, with beams and pillars adorned with brocade.
    • 列筵甚广器用悉是黄金 - The banquet tables were extensive, and all the utensils were made of gold.
    • 阶前有花数丛覆以锦幄 - Before the steps, there were several clusters of flowers covered with brocade canopies.
    • 妓妾俱服纨绮执丝簧善歌舞者至多 - The concubines and maidservants wore fine silk and satin, and there were many who were skilled in music and dance.
    • 客之左右皆有女仆双鬟者二人所须无不必至 - Each guest had two young female servants with double buns who attended to all their needs.
    • 承接之意常日指使者不如芳酒绮肴穷极水陆 - The service was so attentive that even the usual attendants were not as good as the exquisite wine and dishes, which were the best of land and sea.
    • 至于仆乘供给靡不丰盈 - As for the servants, carriages, and supplies, everything was abundant.
    • 自午迄于明晨不覩杯盘狼籍 - From noon until the next morning, there was no sign of the dishes being left in disarray.
    • 朝士云迩后歴观豪 - The court officials said that after observing this, they saw the splendor of the wealthy and powerful.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 钦定四库全书 - A major compilation of Chinese literature, commissioned by the Qing dynasty, which includes a wide range of texts from various periods.
    • 通义坊 - A street name in ancient Chang'an (modern-day Xi'an), known for its noble residences.
    • 刘相国 - Refers to a high-ranking official, likely a duke or marquis, indicating the social status of the residence.
    • 本文宗朝 - During the reign of Emperor Wenzong, providing the historical context.
    • 朔方节度使 - Military governor of Shaofang, a position responsible for defending the northern borders of the Tang dynasty.
    • 李进贤 - A historical figure, likely a notable military leader and statesman.
